impact:
Shares a root cause with ISS-001 (gap A) and ISS-004 (gap D) — drive-dependent clip behaviour. Solve once.

Original: with P6's level error removed, V1E's residual shape error is isolated to two bands at D1.00 — 800Hz (plugin's twin-T notch stays ~11dB deeper than the pedal's; the pedal's notch FILLS IN at drive, the plugin's does not) and 3-4kHz (+7.7dB; the pedal gains only +5.6dB there D0.50->D1.00 vs the plugin's +11.8, i.e. the pedal is already saturated there at D0.50).

2026-07-16 CONFIRMED AND QUANTIFIED (cascade_analysis.py section B, wet-path BL=1.00 captures only, so no blend contamination):
  V1E swing across DRIVE:  twin-T 800Hz 10.30dB (D0.50 -1.1 -> D0.60 -1.2 -> D1.00 -11.4)
                           LF <100Hz  9.10dB (+4.0 -> +3.4 -> -5.1)
                           bridged-T  4.99dB
                           presence/treble 2.5-5k 4.09dB (-0.3 -> -0.1 -> +3.8)
  V2  swing across DRIVE:  twin-T 800Hz 7.03dB (D0.25 +4.3 -> D0.50 +2.1 -> D0.90 -2.8)
                           mid 1-2.5k 3.75dB, LF 3.92dB
KEY: at LOW drive both revisions' notch region is within ~1.2dB — the filters are CORRECT. The error appears ONLY as drive rises. This is a drive/clip-stage fault, not a component value, on BOTH V1E and V2. It also rules out ISS-001's candidate 1 (see that issue).

CAVEAT CARRIED FORWARD: kDriveEndR=8k (P6) is an empirical 8%-of-pot value likely absorbing un-modelled gain limiting at high closed-loop gain (TLC2264 GBW ~0.72MHz -> at gain 101 the closed-loop BW is only ~7kHz). If it IS GBW, the correct model is FREQUENCY-DEPENDENT — which would attack this issue's 3-4kHz residual (a flat resistance cannot) AND is a natural candidate for the drive-dependence seen here. Test the GBW hypothesis before treating 8k as settled.

Unblocked — V1E's captures are all BL=1.00, so this is not affected by ISS-008/ISS-009.
title: V1E drive-dependent band saturation (800Hz notch, 3-4kHz residual)
